title: Bitter Fluffy Festive Irish Vanilla Bean Pilaf
description: A festive and comforting dish featuring bitter flavors such as black tea or coffee combined with fluffy rice pilaf seasoned with vanilla for a unique twist on traditional pilaf.

ingredients.name: Black Tea Bags
ingredients.amount: 4-6 bags (or equal amount of brewed black tea)

ingredients.name: Garlic Cloves
ingredients.amount: 2 cloves (optional for added flavor, omit if egg-free)

instructions: Rinse the rice in cold water and set it aside.
In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water with black tea bags. Bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for about 5 minutes until the tea bags are fully saturated.
Remove the tea bags from the pan and discard them. Stir in the vanilla bean paste, salt, pepper, saffron threads, butter, and optional garlic cloves (if using).
Simmer gently over low heat for about 10 minutes to allow flavors to infuse into the rice water.
Add the rinsed rice to the saucepan with the black tea mixture. Stir well until evenly distributed.

Transfer the mixture to a lightly oiled baking dish and spread it out evenly. Cover tightly with aluminum foil and bake in a preheated 350°F oven for about 45-60 minutes, or until all water is absorbed and rice pilaf is fluffy and tender.
Remove from heat and let cool before serving.
